subject: March Closing - Deadline: April 7, 2026 for your lists of Accruals/FNP
to: Laurence Desroys du Roure </o=ExchangeLabs/ou=Exchange Administrative Group (FYDIBOHF23SPDLT)/cn=Recipients/cn=0dcf763c4ba44098ba7d890e54369bf5-Laurence De>
Maurus de la Rosa </o=ExchangeLabs/ou=Exchange Administrative Group (FYDIBOHF23SPDLT)/cn=Recipients/cn=687fd9993a744c83bc483eb7c80f85ab-b56a7fae-81>
bodyText:
Dear all,

 

In preparation for the March 2026 closing, and as usual now, we need to ensure that all activities that took place in 2026 are recorded in our books as of March 31, 2026.

 

To do so, please contact your service providers to ensure that we receive all the invoices for the services provided in Q1 2026 (or goods received) by Tuesday, April 7th at the latest.

 

As usual, if the invoices related to this period are to be received after this date, please obtain via email from your suppliers the amount (excluding VAT) of their services or deliveries provided in Q1 2026 so that we can record them in ABIVAX accounts as invoices not received (FNP / accruals) in a documented manner.

 

If the supplier is unable to provide this information, please send us the amounts to be accrued by listing the contracts or purchase orders for which the goods or services provided in Q1 2026 have not yet been invoiced.

 

Please provide your results and overall feedback by April 7th at the latest. As much as possible in one single email.

If there is nothing pending and no accrual to book, please let us know as well in this feedback.

 

Also please note we might come back to you with individual invitations when needed.

 

The Finance team (Juliette, Trinidad, Philippe, Frederick, Adrian & Matt) remains at your disposal if you need information (extract of the commitments (“engagé”, budget by supplier/project) or simply need to discuss the subject.

 

Many thanks in advance for your help!

 

Kind regards,

Finance Team

 

 

Bonjour à tous,

 

Pour la clôture de mars 2026, comme vous en avez l’habitude maintenant, nous devons nous assurer que l’ensemble des activités qui ont eu lieu en 2026 sont bien enregistrées dans nos comptes au 31/03/2026.

 

Pour ce faire, merci de vous rapprocher de vos prestataires afin de vous assurer que nous recevrons les factures des prestations effectuées en Q1 2026, au plus tard le mardi 7 avril.

 

Dans le cas où les factures relatives à cette période seraient réceptionnées après cette date, pouvez-vous svp obtenir par mail de vos fournisseurs le montant HT de leurs prestations ou livraisons effectuées sur cette période et non facturées afin que nous puissions les provisionner en factures non parvenues (FNP) de façon documentée. 

 

Si le fournisseur n’est pas en mesure de nous fournir ces informations, veuillez nous transmettre les montants à provisionner en listant les contrats ou bons de commande pour lesquels les biens ou prestations réalisés sur cette période n’ont pas fait l’objet d’une facturation.

 

Autant que possible, merci de nous envoyer l’ensemble des résultats en un seul mail avec la liste des activités réalisées en Q1 2026 et non facturées (FNP) par prestataires, ainsi qu’un récapitulatif pour le mardi 7 avril. 

S’il n’y a pas d’activités ayant eu lieu et non facturées, merci de nous l’indiquer dans ce mail.

 

Enfin, il est possible qu’on revienne vers vous avec des invitations individuelles en cas de besoin.

 

L’équipe Finance (Juliette, Trinidad, Philippe, Frederick, Adrian & Matt) se tient à votre disposition au cas où vous auriez besoin d’information (extraits de l’engagé, budget par fournisseurs/projet) ou simplement besoin d’échanger sur le sujet. 

 

Un grand merci d’avance pour vos retours !

 

Bien à vous,

Finance Team

 

 

 

 



Frederick Golly

Director of Financial Planning & Analysis

+33 7 88 73 14 25

www.abivax.com <http://www.abivax.com/> 

frederick.golly@abivax.com <mailto:frederick.golly@abivax.com>  

	

 

 

From: Frederick Golly 
Sent: mercredi 3 décembre 2025 10:38
To: Josianne Nitcheu <Josianne.Nitcheu@abivax.com>; Pascaline Ploncard <Pascaline.Ploncard@abivax.com>; Laurence Desroys du Roure <Laurence.DesroysDuRoure@abivax.com>; Jessica Huskey <jessica.huskey@abivax.com>; Julien Santo <Julien.Santo@abivax.com>; Didier Scherrer <Di

Subject: ABIVAX x NetSuite : Following up on our meeting

 

Confidential - Oracle Restricted \Including External Recipients

 

Hello Michael,

 

I hope you are doing well.

 

As agreed following our meeting on Tuesday, we are getting back to you with a summary of the offer for the implementation of NetSuite.

 

Please find attached an Excel document that outlines the overall budget, including licenses, support, maintenance, and the implementation services for NetSuite.

 

In addition, for review with your legal team, we have attached the contractual documents: the “Estimates” and the “SSA”, as well as a draft Statement of Work (SOW), which details the services to be delivered by our Customer Success team as part of the implementation.

 

Please feel free to reach out if you have any questions, and we would be happy to schedule a follow-up call to discuss the next steps.

 

Best regards,

Jamal.

 

 <https://www.netsuite.com/portal/home.shtml>

Thank you in advance for your support.

Kind regards,


________________________________

Sent: Monday, March 02, 2026 11:55
To: Girard, Camille <camillegirard@kpmg.fr>; Christophe Hennequin <christophe.hennequin@abivax.com>; Benjamin Talmant <benjamin.talmant@abivax.com>; Jade Nguyen <jade.nguyen-ext@abivax.com>; Trinidad Mesa <trinidad.mesa@abivax.com>; Michael Markman <mike.markman@abivax.com>; Hema Keshava <hema.keshava@abivax.com>
Subject: V3 Availability Poll – [1/2] Master Data Management Workshop 


Hello,

Following up on the workshop focused on organization and governance for effective Master Data Management (MDM).

We haven’t been able to align on a date for the second time yet. We have therefore launched a new poll to collect everyone’s availability.

To schedule the workshop as soon as possible, could you please complete the new poll below as soon as possible and indicate your availability?

This workshop will be conducted in two sessions of 1 hour each. For now, we are planning the first session.

subject: RE: France P2P - Follow-up for ERP planning
bodyText:
Hi Mike,

 

 

I hope you are well.

 

You find enclosed :

 

process flow for invoice recording process (Abivax_Procédure Achat) in English.

 

Kind regards

 



Philippe GONCALVES 
Senior Manager Accounting 

.+33 (0)6 87 66 54 59

www.abivax.com <http://www.abivax.com/> 

philippe.goncalves@abivax.com <mailto:philippe.goncalves@abivax.com> 

	

 

 

De : Trinidad Mesa <Trinidad.Mesa@abivax.com> 
Envoyé : jeudi 26 février 2026 10:33
À : Michael Markman <mike.markman@abivax.com>
Cc : Philippe Goncalves <philippe.goncalves@abivax.com>
Objet : RE: France P2P - Follow-up for ERP planning

 

Hi Mike,

 

As per your request for documentation, you will find enclosed :

- the SOP for PO and contract 

- the approval PO and invoices matrix

- the approval of contract approval (signature policy)

- process flow for invoice recording process (Abivax_Procédure Achat) – in French

-Docushare user guide (for PO)

-Supplier database update and maintenance (called Supplier data base cleaning)

-Recording in currency (saisie en devise) – in French

-Payment process by wire transfer (Moyen de paiement – virements bancaires) – in French

Some description processes are in French, sorry for that…

 

Concerning the session on PTP, are you free on next Monday from 5 p.m. CET?

If not, can you provide with timeslots?

I copy Philippe, who will attend the session.

 

Best

 



Trinidad Mesa 
Finance & Accounting Director

+33 (0)6 51 81 96 04

www.abivax.com <http://www.abivax.com/> 

trinidad.mesa@abivax.com <mailto:trinidad.mesa@abivax.com> 

	

 

 

 

 

De : Michael Markman <mike.markman@abivax.com <mailto:mike.markman@abivax.com> > 
Envoyé : mardi 24 février 2026 16:41
À : Trinidad Mesa <Trinidad.Mesa@abivax.com <mailto:Trinidad.Mesa@abivax.com> >
Objet : France P2P - Follow-up for ERP planning

 

Hi Trinidad - Thank you again for your feedback in the finance discussions so far, it’ very helpful. As I organize the ERP work, I’m trying to build a clear current-state picture of the P2P process (not just vendor demo impressions), so we can make sure the implementation addresses the real operational issues.

 

Would you be open to a short working session (30-45 min) to walk through the current P2P flow on the France side?

 

I’d like to understand, at a practical level:

  - current steps from request/PO through invoice/payment

  - where approvals happen today (and where they do not)

  - key manual workarounds / pain points

  - exceptions or rework points

  - where evidence/audit trail is weak today

  - what you consider “must-have” changes in the future ERP process

 

If you already have any process notes, screenshots, SOPs, flowcharts, checklists, or examples (even informal) that show the current P2P process, please send those first. I’d like to review what exists before we meet so the discussion can stay focused on the real gaps and priorities.

 

Thanks,

Mike
attachmentNames: ABIVAX_Purchase proecss - step 1 to 4 ENG version.pdf

subject: RE: PTP Process + your trip in Paris
bodyText:
Hello Mike, and thank you for your feedback.

 

I think 30 minutes is too short a time to explain the entire purchasing process and the challenges we face.

However, we can use this time to answer your questions once you have finished reading the documentation we sent you:

 

- Purchase Process Steps 1 to 4 (a new version translated into English will be sent to you)

- The Purchase SOP written by Hema, which is complex

- Docushare User Guide: I'm sure you'll have questions about how Docushare works and how we use it in finance to justify POs and invoices.

- The approval matrix authorization: PO validation, invoice validation, according to each person's rank and the amounts involved.

 

I think we should also invite Philippe to this short meeting, as he will have some answers for you. 

What would you like to do? 30 minutes for a few questions prepared in advance, or an hour for a general presentation of the process?

 

Looking forward to hearing from you,

Juliette

Hi Juliette - let’s meet on Tuesday when I’m in the office and we can walk through your current process and challenges you are facing. 

 

Looking forward to working together!

 

Thanks,

Mike

Hi Mike,

Sounds great — I’ve set up a quick half hour on Thursday and am moving Erica to BCC.

Shine and Ken will cover the NetSuite component.

As for SOX, our knowledge of the Company's existing processes and gaps would be incorporated into any system design - and similarly, where there is opportunity for process improvement - which, while not necessarily SOX and largely operations focused, we know there are many bottlenecks and key person dependencies.  Even though I'm a Risk partner/SOX person, I recognize that SOX needs don't always come first (sighs!)

There will also be SOD considerations in roles/p

L’opinion de KPMG, est que la communication non cryptée par l'intermédiaire de l'Internet ne doit pas être considérée comme sécurisée.
Pour cette raison, la politique de KPMG est que l'utilisation de l'Internet pour échanger des informations confidentielles avec ses clients ne doit pas avoir lieu sauf à leur demande. En échangeant de telles informations, le client s'expose à leur divulgation.
Cet e-mail peut contenir des informations confidentielles à l'attention du destinataire. Toute révélation de cette information est strictement interdite et peut être illégale. Si vous avez reçu cet e-mail par erreur, merci de nous en informer immédiatement et supprimez-le.

In KPMG's opinion, non-encrypted communication via the Internet is not to be considered secure.
For that reason, it is KPMG's policy that uninvited use of the Internet concerning exchange of confidential information with our clients must not take place.
When exchanging information, the client is held liable. This e-mail may contain confidential information and is intended solely for the addressee, and any disclosure of this information is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this e-mail by mistake, please notify us immediately and delete this mail.

Mentions légale
